channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Council Worker Salaries: A Comparison with Prime Ministerial Pay
item.description: A Sky News investigation, reportedly focusing on the salaries of local council employees, has revealed that a significant number receive remuneration exceeding that of the Prime Minister. The report a...

item.title: Kenyan-made climate camera set for International Space Station arrival on Monday evening
item.description: A Kenyan-led scientific payload known as ClimCam is set to arrive at the International Space Station (ISS) on Monday evening following a successful launch into space on Saturday at 1.41pm East African...

item.title: Britney Spears voluntarily enters rehab following DUI arrest and upcoming court appearance
item.description: Pop icon Britney Spears has voluntarily checked herself into a rehabilitation facility following her recent arrest on suspicion of driving under the influence. According to a representative for the si...

item.title: US-Iran tensions escalate amid diplomatic breakdown and Hormuz Strait threats
item.description: This editorial discusses the increasingly fragile state of US-Iran relations, highlighting how diplomatic efforts are being undermined by aggressive rhetoric and political brinkmanship. According to t...

item.title: Evergrande founder admits multiple financial crimes including fraud and bribery in Chinese court
item.description: Hui Ka Yan, the founder of the heavily indebted Chinese property developer China Evergrande, has pleaded guilty to a series of serious financial crimes, according to a statement from a mainland Chines...

item.title: The Instrumentalisation of Life's Joys: Prioritising Utility over Intrinsic Value
item.description: Philosopher Julian Baggini critiques the pervasive instrumentalisation of intrinsically valuable activities such as art, sex, nature, and social bonds, where they are promoted primarily for extrinsic ...

item.title: Safeguarding South Africa’s diamond jobs amid global and local pressures
item.description: South Africa’s diamond sector, once a cornerstone of the national economy, is now under severe pressure from a combination of global and domestic challenges. Geopolitical tensions, notably the ongoing...

item.title: Pemmy Majodina: Executing South Africa’s Plan for Water Security and Accountability
item.description: In her opinion piece, Minister of Water and Sanitation Pemmy Majodina stresses that South Africa’s water security is not just a developmental concern but a matter of national security. She recalls tha...

item.title: Ramil Madriaga testifies in House impeachment probe amid alleged links to Vice President Sara Duterte

item.title: New CAQ leader Christine Fréchette faces pressure to rebuild party ahead of Quebec election
item.description: Christine Fréchette, newly elected leader of Quebec’s Coalition Avenir Québec (CAQ) and premier-designate, is entering office at a critical and challenging moment for her party. With provincial electi...

item.title: Addressing Kenya’s Growing Gambling Addiction Crisis
item.description: A serious social crisis is unfolding across Kenya, as gambling addiction silently destroys families and livelihoods. The editorial highlights that betting has become as damaging as alcoholism and drug...

item.title: Historical Reflections on Western Imperialism and Current Middle Eastern Diplomacy

item.description: Former Conservative cabinet minister Peter MacKay has described the recent wave of floor-crossings on Parliament Hill as a significant “body blow” to the Conservative Party, as reports of Liberal effo...

item.title: Australia joins diplomatic talks on Strait of Hormuz security amid ongoing conflict, rules out immediate naval deployment
item.description: Australia will take part in UK and France-led international talks this week focused on securing the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping route, but is not expected to deploy naval support unti...

item.title: UP minister alleges Pakistan-linked conspiracy behind Noida workers’ protest amid violence and wage demands
item.description: A massive workers’ protest in Noida, Uttar Pradesh, escalated into widespread disruption, traffic chaos, and sporadic violence, prompting strong political reactions and security concerns. The protests...

item.title: Government spending contradicts Ruto’s austerity agenda
item.description: President William Ruto came to power in 2022 with a firm vow to push austerity measures, aiming to reduce Kenya’s ballooning public debt and control government wastage. His administration initially in...

item.title: Man arrested by ACT Police a day after release from NSW prison
item.description: A 34-year-old man who had just been released from a New South Wales prison on Sunday was arrested the following day by ACT Policing officers. The man is accused of various offences dating back to 2025...

item.title: Donald Trump Criticizes Remarks Made by the Pope
item.description: The article reportedly covered a public exchange of comments between then U.S. presidential candidate Donald Trump and Pope Francis. The story emerged after the Pope expressed concerns about political...

item.title: Australia expands domestic missile production amid global arms demand and supply chain challenges
item.description: Australia is advancing efforts to establish a sovereign missile industry, marking a significant shift in its defence strategy as global military spending rises. For the first time in decades, the coun...

item.title: Pakistan reviews auto taxes to make vehicles affordable for middle-class buyers
item.description: The Pakistani government is considering major changes in auto sector taxation and duties under a new auto policy, aimed at making cars more affordable for the middle class. According to Engineering De...
